_description: LTG Warmth Guarantees — canonical list of character palette prefixes whose entries must remain unambiguously warm (R >= G and R >= B, within soft_tolerance). Used by LTG_TOOL_palette_warmth_lint_v004.py and the CI warmth gate. Supersedes warmth_lint_config.json warm_prefixes for production use.
_note_char_l: Only CHAR-L hoodie entries appear in the palette table format (CHAR-L-04, CHAR-L-08, CHAR-L-11). Other CHAR-L entries (skin, jeans, shoes) are in prose format only and are intentionally excluded from machine warmth checking — jeans are blue (B>R), shoes are near-white. The warmth lint tool's table-row regex ensures only table-format entries are checked.
_note_char_c: Only CHAR-C skin tone entries appear in the palette table format (CHAR-C-01, CHAR-C-02, CHAR-C-03). Cosmo's cardigan (dusty lavender, RW-08, B>R) and shirt stripes (cerulean, B>R) are intentionally cool and are NOT warm-guaranteed. Only skin base, skin shadow, and skin highlight are warm-guaranteed. The warmth lint table-row regex ensures only table-format entries are checked.

REAL.description: Real World interior — lamp-lit warm scene with cyan monitor accent. SF01 archetype. Warm/cool separation of at least 12 PIL units (0-255 hue scale) is expected.

GLITCH.description: Glitch World / night storm — cold dominant, warm building-window accents only. SF02 archetype. Near-zero warm/cool separation is CORRECT. Warm presence (excess) is a flag.

OTHER_SIDE.description: Other Side (CRT world) — zero warm tones allowed. SF03/SF04 archetype. Any warm presence in the frame is a production error. warm_cool_threshold=0 means the check passes only when warm is absent.

_world_presets_note: Thresholds are used by render_qa._check_warm_cool() when --world-type is passed. warmth_lint_v004 --world-type outputs the threshold for Kai Nakamura's CI gate integration. Coordinate: Kai Nakamura to add --world-type to LTG_TOOL_render_qa_v001.py.
